task.title: Instructions
task.shortdesc: The Oscilloscope screen is divided into 4 sections – 1st green line to the left

step.cmd: Before starting the work instruction activity, perform the following steps:

substep.cmd: Do not begin these tasks without confirmation that personnel directly involved have completed the required training associated with the work instruction activity and have reviewed this document.
Gather necessary tools and equipment as identified below in the Equipment Section.
Wear and use additional Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) and safety equipment required for this specific work instruction.
Obey all applicable safety requirements and procedures.
Notify appropriate personnel of the activity and estimated time needed to complete the tasks.

step.cmd: To setup the container spacing, perform the following steps:

substep.cmd: The HMI screen of the Multi 4 is located on the front right door of the machine.
Stop the flow of containers into the machine before the spacer.
From the main screen on the HMI, Click on the Article button on the menu bar and login to the software using the assigned password.
Select Management
To Load a job, perform one of the following steps:
Select “New” to create a new job with default settings if no job history is available.
Select “duplicate”to copy a similar job to a new record.
Click on an existing job history for job to run.
Press the parameters button. Enter the container specifications if necessary.
Save and close.
If needed, adjust or replace the panel at the infeed based on the size of the container to reduce the amount of stray light that will enter the machine.
Adjust and confirm proper spacing of the containers entering into the machine using the spacing device or by adjusting the MCAL conveyor speed.
There should be a minimum of 1.5 container width spacing for MCAL4
Confirm that the container’s position is consistently centered on the conveyor when it releases from the spacer.
The objective is to center the flow of containers to the cameras to have the same images sizes on the screen.
At production speeds, look through the spacing device to visually confirm the containers are stable on the conveyor at production speeds. If the containers are not stable, check the following items:
Level the conveyer
Adjust the conveyer chain until it is running smoothly and there is no jerking motion.
Adjust the spacing belts until the container is centered on the conveyor.

step.cmd: To set up sidewall inspection, perform the following steps:

substep.cmd: If the conveyor is running, Stop the flow of containers into the machine.
Access the Adjustments menu by pressing the Adjustments button on the main screen.
Select the Optical adjustments
In the Source color frame, select the type of the light source installed on your machine, Infrared or White.
Press the BODY button at the top right of the screen.
In the view selection, select thumbnail images 2 or 5 to make the desired light source flash.
Place a setup container on the conveyer and while viewing the image on the screen, center the container in front of the center camera and the flashing light source.
Adjust the light level of body camera using the oscilloscope.
The Oscilloscope is activated in the center by default.
Adjust the brightness so that brightness range in the sidewall area as shown by the Oscilloscope trace is between 50% and 75%.

info.p: The Oscilloscope screen is divided into 4 sections – 1st green line to the left represents 25%, middle red line represents 50%, next green line to the right of the middle is 75%.

substep.cmd: The brightness level can be fine-tuned with individual rows to achieve uniform lighting throughout the container image.
Press the SHOULDER button at the top right of the screen.
In the view selection, select thumbnail image 5 to make the desired light source flash.
Place a setup container on the conveyor in the center of the flashing light source.
Adjust the height of the cameras with the adjusting wheel so that the container is displayed correctly in the image.
Using the mouse, position the gauge line level with the highest point of the container.
Repeat the above step for thumbnail image 6.
Use the same steps to set the camera height for the camera’s on the left column.
When the adjustments are complete for the right and left columns, read the height which is displayed in millimeters on the 2 adjusting wheels. These 2 values must be equal.
Enter this value via the keypad on the Camera height line. This value will be saved in the item record and will make future adjustments easier.
Adjust overall brightness with the target of between 50 and 75%.
Do not move the slider within the body or the body settings will be incorrect.
To continue with the setup of sidewall inspection, refer to work instruction INS-03-052 Device Setup – Tiama – Sidewall for MCAL 4.

step.cmd: To setup the distance, perform the following steps:

substep.cmd: Open the Distances Button
Start the conveyor
Pass one container thru the machine, ensuring it is centered on the conveyor.
Adjust the blue center line on images 2 and 5.
Check the following parameters:

info.p: The number of Gauge is 4 or 5

substep.cmd: Repeat until the blue line is centered on the container.
Place a setup container with a defect into the machine.
Physically stand in front of the reject blower and observe reject timing.
Adjust the distance to the reject device if necessary.
Ejector 1 Distance
Ejector 2 Distance
Blowing time (in ms)
Adjust reject pressure and number of adjustable blower holes when necessary.
Confirm the container is being rejected at the center of the sidewall.
Repeat until the container is properly rejected.
At the start of production, check that the correct container is being rejected and not affecting other containers. If not properly rejecting, repeat section on blowing time and pressure.
To continue with the setup of stress inspection, refer to work instruction INS-03-062 Device Setup – Tiama – Stress for MCAL 4.

step.cmd: Upon completion of tasks, perform the following steps:

substep.cmd: Confirm the designated work area is clear and communicate to personnel the intent to restart energy.
Perform basic housekeeping duties. Clean up the work space, tools and equipment, dispose of trash, put tools and equipment in assigned area.
Notify appropriate personnel that the work instruction activity has been completed.
Record and report findings and results.
Follow appropriate instructions for notification of findings and results relating to specifications, targets and/or reaction limits.
